signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.03 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Strait of Hormuz blockade headlines. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded. While VIX at 17.0 indicates normal volatility, the $35B BoJ intervention last week and the 160.00 psychological level define the ceiling. Escalation in Iran conflict introduces immediate intervention risk on any move toward 160.00 as Japan markets remain on holiday.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Retail Divergence + RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7208 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 90% short creates a massive institutional-retail divergence. RBA at 4.10% (Hold) vs Fed 3.75% (Hold) provides a +35bp carry advantage for AUD. With RBA hike expectations rising ahead of the 5 May meeting and Hormuz tensions supporting energy-linked AUD, retail capitulation risk is high. Bullish extension targets 0.7300 if RBA signals a hawkish shift.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) shows specs are heavily fading the RBNZ's hawkish bias. RBNZ at 2.25% faces a market pricing a hike for the 27 May meeting. This positioning-policy mismatch creates significant short-squeeze risk if energy-driven inflation forces the RBNZ's hand. NZD/USD at 0.5900 is testing a critical psychological floor; a failure to break lower despite the -150bp carry deficit vs USD suggests spec exhaustion.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry Headwinds
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1723 — Correlation with EuroStoxx has shifted to +0.727 (z=+1.89σ), diverging from the -0.138 norm. EUR is now trading as a pure risk-proxy rather than a funding currency. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% provides a +175bp carry advantage to USD, structurally limiting EUR upside. COT LF net short -18,293 confirms institutional preference for USD carry. Watch the 30 Apr ECB meeting for any shift in the 'Hold' bias to reprice the spread.
